description: Cut time-to-first-correct-task-mgr-call for fresh-context agents from ~5 wrong invocations to <=1. Add `task-mgr current` (resolved-context probe), `task-mgr cheatsheet` (curated recipes + clap-introspection-generated reference + drift CI), `task-mgr how` (intent advisor), wrong-name/wrong-flag hint table on clap parse errors, and hard-refuse `task-mgr add --depended-on-by <id>` when the target lives in a different PRD than the active prefix. Complementary to the CLI-fix plan in /home/chris/.claude/plans/task-mgr-task-mgmt-ergonomics.md (which adds remove/update/--from-json on add). Plan: /home/chris/.claude/plans/plan-out-how-to-lazy-pixel.md.

criteria: No warnings in `cargo check` output (scoped to touched crate)
No warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` (scoped to touched crate)
All scoped tests pass with `cargo test -p task-mgr <module-or-fn>`
No breaking changes to existing CLI subcommands or flags unless documented in task description
Every new subcommand is registered in `src/cli/commands.rs` (enum), `src/main.rs` (dispatch), AND `src/handlers.rs` (impl) per learning 1405
Tests that read CLI state must `Command::env_remove("TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX")` per learning 2644/2903
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION - ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE - Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS - Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY - Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit - qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Phase 2 foundation - prefer solutions that lay strong post-launch foundations (1:10+ savings ratio)
Edge cases = test cases - every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at REVIEW-001 - REVIEW must leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Ship working code with tests to prove it; handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
Abstractions with only one concrete use
Error messages that don't identify what went wrong or what to do about it
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context
Hand-written documentation that duplicates clap-discoverable information (defeats Prong 2 drift CI)
Subprocess invocations of `task-mgr --help` from tests or runtime code (use clap::CommandFactory per learning 160)

acceptanceCriteria:
Positive: `task-mgr current` prints exactly `active prefix=

source= target=` on one line and exits 0
Positive: `task-mgr current` with no resolvable PRD prints `no active PRD; pass --from-json or set TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX` and exits 0 (probe, not error)
Positive: `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by <id> '{...}'` emits a leading `→ active prefix=... source=... target=...` stderr line BEFORE any DB write or warning
Unit test: ResolutionSource::EnvVar when env var set and matches a known prefix
Unit test: ResolutionSource::SinglePrefix when env empty and DB has exactly one prefix
Unit test: ResolutionSource::None when env empty and DB has zero or 2+ prefixes
Unit test: `task-mgr current` against an empty DB exits 0 with the 'no active PRD' message
Negative: read-only commands (show, list, next) do NOT emit the resolved-context stderr line - assert no `→ active prefix` substring in their stderr
Known-bad: a naive impl that prints the stderr line AFTER any warning would pass a 'line present' check. Test must read stderr line 1 (first non-empty) and assert it starts with `→ active prefix=`

CONTRACT: ResolvedContext struct fields { prefix: String, source: ResolutionSource, prd_json_path: PathBuf } - grep both src/commands/add.rs and src/commands/current.rs to confirm matching access (no parallel struct)

Failure mode: if TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX is set to a value not in any known PRD - exit non-zero with `Stale TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX=<x>; known prefixes: <list>` (preserve current add.rs behavior; verify regression)
Tests use `Command::env_remove("TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX")` per learning 2644/2903
Commit message: `feat: FEAT-001-completed - task-mgr current + ResolvedContext stderr on writes`
userStories.description:
Promote the currently-implicit resolution of active prefix + target PRD JSON to a first-class `ResolvedContext { prefix: String, source: ResolutionSource, prd_json_path: PathBuf }`. Emit it as a leading stderr line on `add` (any write op that resolves prefix). Add new `task-mgr current` read-only subcommand that prints the same struct.

DO: reuse `resolve_active_prefix` in src/commands/add.rs:305-350 - wrap, don't replace. Add a `ResolutionSource` enum (EnvVar, SinglePrefix, FromJsonFlag, None) so the source is observable. New subcommand follows the 6-file shape from learning 1405.

DO NOT: emit the stderr line on read-only commands (show, list, next) - keeps quiet output for the hot path. Do NOT re-implement resolution logic in current.rs - it must call the same helper used by add.

Edge cases:
- Empty DB: current exits 0 with 'no active PRD; pass --from-json or set TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX'
- Multi-prefix DB, env unset: ResolutionSource::None, suggest setting env var
- env var set to non-existent prefix: exit non-zero with 'Stale TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX=<x>; known prefixes: ...' (preserve current add.rs behavior)
- env var with case mismatch: must NOT auto-match - exact comparison

Known-bad to guard against:
- Printing the stderr line AFTER 'Added task' would pass any 'line present' assertion but the agent already missed the chance to abort. The test must assert line position == 1.
- Duplicating resolution: two call sites doing their own env lookup would drift. Test must assert a single resolve_active_prefix call per command invocation (use a counter via DI or trace).
edgeCases: Empty DB: ResolutionSource::None, exit 0 with hint
Multi-prefix DB with env unset: ResolutionSource::None (cannot auto-pick)
env var set to non-existent prefix: exit non-zero with 'Stale TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX=<x>'
env var with case mismatch (lowercase vs uppercase prefix): NO auto-match - exact comparison required
PRD JSON path resolves to a file that no longer exists on disk: print path anyway with stderr warning 'path does not exist on disk'

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Exit non-zero with 'Stale TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX=<x>; known prefixes: <list>' (preserves current add.rs behavior - regression check required)

failureModes.cause: DB connection fails during current invocation
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Exit non-zero with the DB error, do NOT print partial context

userStories.notes: Learning [2644]/[2903]: clean env var in tests via Command::env_remove. Learning [1405]: new top-level subcommand spans 6 files - src/cli/commands.rs (enum variant), src/main.rs (dispatch), src/handlers.rs (impl wire-up), src/commands/current.rs (impl), src/cli/tests.rs (exhaustive match), tests for the new command. Learning [1069]: clap enum extension breaks exhaustive matches - update tests/cli_tests.rs. Plan: Prong 3a. Reuse: src/commands/add.rs:305-350 resolve_active_prefix is the lift point.
qualityDimensions: Resolution happens exactly once per command invocation (no duplicate prefix lookups across helper calls)
Stderr context is emitted via a single function call from a shared helper - no copy-paste of formatting between commands
`task-mgr current` is read-only - does not open a write transaction or touch DB connection write paths
ResolutionSource enum is exhaustive and named for the SOURCE not the OUTCOME (EnvVar/SinglePrefix/FromJsonFlag/None - not 'Auto'/'Manual')
Tests are hermetic - use Command::env_remove per learning 2644/2903; never depend on the host shell's env
userStories.title: task-mgr current + ResolvedContext stderr on writes

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `task-mgr cheatsheet` exits 0 with sections in order: `## Common Recipes` (curated), `## Command Reference` (generated table), `## State Inspection` (one paragraph pointing to `task-mgr current`)
Positive: `task-mgr enhance show` output contains the SAME generated reference table as `task-mgr cheatsheet` (substring match)
Positive: src/cli/introspect.rs exposes a single `fn generate_command_reference() -> String` consumed by cheatsheet, enhance/templates, and tests/cheatsheet_drift.rs
Drift test: tests/cheatsheet_drift.rs enumerates clap::CommandFactory::command().get_subcommands() and asserts every non-hidden subcommand appears in generate_command_reference() output
Drift test: for every `--flag` mentioned in the curated recipes block, asserts the flag exists on its named subcommand (parses the recipe markdown for `task-mgr <cmd> ... --<flag>` patterns)
Drift test: introducing a `set-status` reference into the curated recipes block (without adding a real `set-status` subcommand) causes the test to fail with a clear diff
Negative: the strings `set-status`, `add --from-json`, `recall --top-k`, `learnings show <N>` MUST NOT appear in `task-mgr cheatsheet` stdout (assert with grep)
Negative: cheatsheet/enhance/drift test MUST NOT spawn `task-mgr --help` as a subprocess - grep for `Command::new("task-mgr")` returns no hits in their source

Known-bad: an impl that hand-codes the reference table as a second &'static str would pass a 'cheatsheet renders' check. Drift test must reject this by comparing the table's subcommand set to CommandFactory's set
CONTRACT: enhance/templates.rs uses generate_command_reference() at render time (not concatenated at compile time as another literal) - grep enhance/templates.rs for the function call
Failure mode: if CommandFactory returns zero subcommands (degenerate test state) - cheatsheet renders curated section only with a stderr warning, no panic
qualityDimensions met: drift CI runs in <100ms hermetic (no fs, no subprocess), curated recipes <=30 lines
Commit message: `feat: FEAT-002-completed - task-mgr cheatsheet + clap-introspection drift CI`
userStories.description:
One canonical, always-accurate cheat sheet. Split WORKFLOW_TEMPLATE (currently a 175-line `&'static str` in src/commands/enhance/templates.rs:26-200) into (a) curated narrative + recipes, (b) a generated command/flag reference table walking `clap::Command::get_subcommands()`. Expose via new `task-mgr cheatsheet` subcommand AND consume in `task-mgr enhance agents` so the marker block in CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md uses the generated table. Add `tests/cheatsheet_drift.rs` as the CI gate that prevents docs from referencing non-existent commands or flags.

DO: use clap::CommandFactory (per learning 160) - no subprocess spawning. Write the generator into a new `src/cli/introspect.rs` module so cheatsheet, enhance/templates, and the drift test all consume the SAME source. Curated recipes section is a short `&'static str` literal (the 'I want to ... -> run ...' map for the top 10 ops).

DO NOT: regenerate documentation prose from clap output - keep narrative hand-written. Clap can't capture the WHY; only the WHAT. Don't hand-code a second copy of the reference table for cheatsheet - that's the drift bug we're closing.

Edge cases:
- Subcommand with empty after_help: render with about line only (no example)
- Subcommand with multi-line about: take first line, ellipsize rest
- Nested subcommands (loop init, loop run): render the leaf path (e.g. 'loop init'), not just 'loop'
- Hidden subcommands: exclude from generated reference

Known-bad:
- A hand-coded reference table in cheatsheet.rs would pass a 'table renders' test but reintroduce drift. Drift test must enumerate clap::CommandFactory::command().get_subcommands() and assert the table contains every non-hidden one and no extras.
- Spawning `task-mgr --help` as a subprocess to populate the reference - violates learning 160 and adds binary-on-PATH coupling.
edgeCases: Subcommand with empty after_help: render about line only, no example
Subcommand with multi-line about: first line, ellipsize
Nested subcommands (loop init, loop run): render leaf path, not just parent
Hidden subcommands: exclude from generated reference
Subcommand whose about is missing entirely: render name with `(no description)` placeholder

failureModes.cause: CommandFactory returns zero subcommands (degenerate clap state in tests)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Render curated section only, emit stderr warning, do not panic
Use the raw text as-is; do not panic; do not silently drop

userStories.notes:
Learning [160]: clap::CommandFactory::command().get_subcommands() is the correct introspection entry. Learning [1069]: cli/tests.rs exhaustive match will break when the new Cheatsheet enum variant is added. Learning [1405]: 6+ file shape - mirror it. Learning [2329]: backwards-compat pattern N/A here (pure addition). Learning [2797]: confirms 'verify docs against compiled binary' is the right pattern. Plan: Prong 2 + Prong 3b. Reuse: src/commands/enhance/templates.rs WORKFLOW_TEMPLATE macro composition pattern.

## Curated cheat-sheet starter list (deconfliction feed, 2026-05-18)

Use this as the source-of-truth set for the FEAT-002 curated-recipes block and to seed the generated reference. Drop anything that doesn't fit the <=30-line budget. The "negative anchors" list is the smoking-gun set the audit removed from CLAUDE.md — re-introducing any of these strings into cheatsheet stdout should be a drift-test failure.

Curated recipes (intent → command):

- Look up one task: `task-mgr show <task-id>`
- List tasks (filter): `task-mgr list` + `--status` / `--prefix` / `--task-type`
- Pick next eligible task + claim: `task-mgr next --claim` (with `--prefix $PREFIX` in loop)
- Add a new task (fixup / follow-up): `echo '{…}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by <CONTRACT-id-or-milestone>`
- Mark status from a loop iteration: emit `<task-status>TASK-ID:done</task-status>` (done / failed / skipped / irrelevant / blocked)
- Mark status outside the loop: `task-mgr complete | fail | skip | unblock | unskip | reset <task-id>` — NO `set-status`
- Record a learning: `task-mgr learn --outcome <success|failure|workaround|pattern> --title "…" --tags "…"`
- Recall learnings for THIS task: `task-mgr recall --for-task <task-id>`
- Recall by query: `task-mgr recall --query "<text>"` (`--allow-degraded` for offline)
- Initialize DB from a PRD JSON: `task-mgr loop init <prd>.json` — mid-loop sync: `--append --update-existing`
- Run the autonomous loop: `task-mgr loop run <prd>.json --yes`
- Run multiple PRDs: `task-mgr batch init '…/*.json' && task-mgr batch run '…/*.json' --yes`
- List architectural decisions: `task-mgr decisions list`
- Ratify a decision: `task-mgr decisions resolve <id> <letter>`
- Edit / tag / supersede a learning: `task-mgr edit-learning <id> --add-tags / --remove-tags / --supersedes <src-id>`
- Apply / invalidate a learning: `task-mgr apply-learning <id>` / `task-mgr invalidate-learning <id>`

Negative anchors (must NOT appear in cheatsheet stdout — clap-verified wrong on 2026-05-18):

- `task-mgr set-status` — no such subcommand. Real verbs: complete / fail / skip / unblock / unskip / reset. Inside a loop: `<task-status>` tag.
- `task-mgr learn-tag` — no such subcommand. Use `edit-learning --add-tags` and/or `--supersedes`.
- `task-mgr add --from-json` — `--from-json` is on `init`, not on `add`. `add` accepts `--stdin` / `--json`.
- `task-mgr recall --top-k` / `--list-tags` / `--prefix` — none exist. Use `--limit` / `--tags <single>` / `--for-task`.
- `task-mgr decisions list --tag` — no such flag. Use `--format json` and filter externally.
- `task-mgr learnings show <N>` — no such subcommand. Use `task-mgr learnings --recent N`.

Two complementary drift gates (orthogonal — do NOT bundle into FEAT-002):

- FEAT-002's hermetic `tests/cheatsheet_drift.rs` gates renderer-vs-CLI: "generator never falls behind the clap tree". This is in-scope.
- A future `task-mgr enhance agents --check` would gate file-on-disk-vs-renderer: "CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md never falls behind the generator". Different failure class; out of scope for this PRD. Ship as a small follow-up after FEAT-002 lands.
qualityDimensions: Drift CI is hermetic: no subprocess, no fs reads, runs in <100ms (learning 160 pattern)
Curated recipes section is concise (<=30 lines): the top 10 operations only - if it grows past 30 lines, split into separate doc
Generated table includes `about` blurb + 1 example per subcommand (pulled from after_help first non-empty line)
All three consumers (cheatsheet, enhance/templates, drift test) call the SAME generate_command_reference() function - single source of truth, enforced by grep test
introspect.rs has no dependency on commands/ - lives in src/cli/ to avoid circular deps
userStories.title: task-mgr cheatsheet + clap-introspection-generated reference + drift CI

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: `task-mgr set-status FOO done` exits non-zero, stderr contains BOTH clap's 'unrecognized subcommand' line AND a `hint: task-mgr has no `set-status` subcommand. To change status:` worked transition table
Positive: `task-mgr recall --top-k 5` exits non-zero, stderr contains `hint: task-mgr recall has no `--top-k`. Use `--limit <N>`.`
Positive: `task-mgr learnings show 2236` exits non-zero, stderr contains `hint:` suggesting `task-mgr recall --query ... --limit 1`
Positive: `task-mgr remove FOO` exits non-zero, stderr contains `hint:` suggesting `task-mgr irrelevant FOO --reason '...'`
Positive: `task-mgr update FOO-1 --title x` exits non-zero, stderr contains `hint:` referencing the JSON-edit + loop-init-update-existing workflow
Unit test (table-driven): for each (invocation, expected_hint_substring) pair, run via assert_cmd::Command and assert stderr contains the substring
Negative: `task-mgr --foo` (random unknown flag, NOT in hints table) produces clap's default error with NO `hint:` line - assert stderr does not contain 'hint:'
Negative: `task-mgr inits` (typo close to `init`, NOT in hints table) produces clap's default 'did you mean init' with NO `hint:` line

Negative: hints must not lie - `task-mgr remove` hint MUST suggest `irrelevant`, not a future `remove` command. Hardcoded fact-check test asserts the literal string `irrelevant` appears in the remove hint
Known-bad: an impl that prints hints on EVERY error would spam normal usage. The negative tests above guard this; specifically assert random-flag and typo-close-to-real-command produce no hint

qualityDimensions met: hint tables are pure data (grep `if`/`match` in error_recovery.rs lookup - <5 control-flow keywords); per-subcommand scoping (wrong-flag hints check the failing subcommand path, not just flag name)

Failure mode: if error_recovery code panics during formatting - fallback to clap's default unmodified; assert via deliberate panic injection test that the binary still exits non-zero with clap's message
Commit message: `feat: FEAT-003-completed - wrong-name/flag hint table on clap parse errors`
userStories.description:
Wrap clap's parse error surface so that known-wrong invocations carry a worked example. Static WRONG_NAME_HINTS and WRONG_FLAG_HINTS maps cover the patterns observed in the failure trace.

DO: define both tables as `static [(name, hint); N]` arrays in a new src/cli/error_recovery.rs. Initial coverage:
- `set-status` -> status-transition table (complete/fail/skip/reset/unblock/unskip/irrelevant)
- `remove` / `delete` -> `task-mgr irrelevant <id> --reason ...` (cross-reference the CLI-fix plan that will add `remove`)
- `update` / `edit` / `change` -> 'no `update` subcommand yet - edit the JSON in the worktree, then `task-mgr loop init <prd>.json --append --update-existing`' (cross-reference the other plan)
- `learnings show <N>` / `learnings get <N>` -> 'use `task-mgr recall --query <text> --limit 1` or `task-mgr learnings | grep <N>`'
- `recall --top-k` -> 'no `--top-k`. Use `--limit <N>`.'

DO NOT: replace clap's normal error path - WRAP it. Clap's normal error message still prints; our `hint: ...` goes on a separate trailing stderr line.

Edge cases:
- Typo close to a real command but not in hints table (`task-mgr inits`): fall through to clap's built-in 'did you mean' with no extra hint
- Subcommand with `--top-k` style flag that DOES exist elsewhere: verify wrong-flag hints are scoped per-subcommand, not global
- ANSI color codes around clap's error output: don't break our hint placement; hint goes on its own line after clap's reset sequence

Known-bad:
- A hint engine that prints hints on EVERY clap error would spam normal usage (e.g. typing `task-mgr --foo` for a true typo). Test asserts `task-mgr --foo` produces NO hint, just clap's default.
- A 'remove' hint that lies by suggesting a non-existent `task-mgr remove` command (post-CLI-fix-plan, this becomes real - but until then, suggest `irrelevant`)
edgeCases: Typo close to a real command but not in hints table: fall through to clap's 'did you mean' with no extra hint
Wrong-flag for a flag name that exists on a DIFFERENT subcommand: scope to the failing subcommand, don't suggest the unrelated one
ANSI color codes in clap's error output: hint placement does not break formatting
Recovery fn itself panics: fall back to clap's unmodified default

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Catch via std::panic::catch_unwind, fall back to clap's default error path, log to stderr

userStories.notes: Plan: Prong 4a. Addresses the specific wrong invocations from the failure trace (see plan Context section). Learning [2797] supports the 'lying docs cause real failures' rationale. This hint table is the safety net for the moment a wrong invocation slips through despite Prong 2 docs accuracy. Reuse: assert_cmd::Command pattern used elsewhere in tests/cli_tests.rs.
qualityDimensions: Hint tables are pure data - no branching logic in lookup beyond exact-match on string key
Wrong-flag hints are scoped per-subcommand (look at the path that failed, not global)
Hints are short: <=5 lines, with a copy-pasteable corrected example
Adding a new entry is a single-line change to the static array - no schema migration, no new fn
Error recovery is wrapper-shaped: clap's print happens first, hint appended after; no replacement
userStories.title: Wrong-name / wrong-flag hint table on clap parse errors

acceptanceCriteria: Positive (how): `task-mgr how 'change task status'` exits 0, stdout contains 'complete', 'fail', 'skip', 'reset' as a transition table
Positive (how): `task-mgr how 'add a follow-up task'` exits 0, stdout contains the three canonical `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by ...` forms
Positive (how): `task-mgr how 'where will my add land'` exits 0, stdout points to `task-mgr current`
Positive (how): `task-mgr how 'totally unrelated nonsense query'` exits 0 with `no recipe matched. Run \`task-mgr cheatsheet\` for the full surface.`
Positive (how): `task-mgr how` with no arg lists available intent keyword bags, one per line, exits 0
Positive (how): `task-mgr how` appears in `task-mgr --help` output - it is a VISIBLE subcommand, not hidden

Positive (refusal): `TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX=other-prefix task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by 5ba153a7-MILESTONE-FINAL '{...}'` exits non-zero, stderr contains `Refusing: target ... lives in PRD <other>.json (prefix 5ba153a7), but active prefix is other-prefix`

Positive (refusal): refusal message contains BOTH suggested fixes: `TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX=5ba153a7 task-mgr add ...` AND a reference to `--from-json tasks/<correct-prd>.json` (when that flag ships from the CLI-fix plan)
Positive (refusal): same call with `TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX=5ba153a7` succeeds as today - regression check
Unit test (refusal): pre-flight check runs BEFORE the DB transaction opens - verify by injecting a DB-failure mock and confirming the refusal still fires (no DB error wrapper around it)
Unit test (refusal): assert DB row count and target JSON file mtime are unchanged after a refusal (no partial writes)
Known-bad (refusal): an impl that runs the check AFTER the DB write would pass 'error emitted' tests but leak a stray row. Row-count and mtime assertions guard this
CONTRACT: cross-PRD detection calls FEAT-001's `resolve_active_prefix` and ResolvedContext - grep src/commands/add.rs for both names (single source of truth, no parallel resolution)

Failure mode: --depended-on-by references an id whose prefix matches NO known PRD - suggest `task-mgr list --prefix

` to find the right id, refuse

Commit message: `feat: FEAT-004-completed - task-mgr how + hard-refuse cross-PRD --depended-on-by`
userStories.description:
Two related changes that share the cross-PRD detection logic.

(a) New visible `task-mgr how '<intent>'` subcommand: hand-curated keyword -> recipe map covering the 10 most common intents. NOT LLM-powered - static, deterministic, fast.

(b) Hard-refusal logic in `task-mgr add` when `--depended-on-by <id>` resolves to a PRD JSON different from the active prefix's PRD. Current behavior silently writes to the wrong file (learning 2236/2281); this change makes it an explicit error with a worked fix.

DO: define `INTENTS: &[(&[&str], &str)]` in src/commands/intents.rs - array of (keyword bag, recipe text) tuples. Matching = case-insensitive `contains_all` on tokens. Cover top 10 intents:
  1. change task status (complete/fail/skip/reset/unblock/unskip/irrelevant)
  2. add follow-up task (three canonical --depended-on-by forms)
  3. spawn fix from REVIEW (--depended-on-by REVIEW-001 with FIX-xxx id)
  4. sync JSON into a running effort (loop init --append --update-existing)
  5. ratify a tm-decision (`tm-decisions resolve <id> <letter>`)
  6. recall learnings for a task (recall --for-task <id>)
  7. find tasks by file (list --touches-file <path>)
  8. debug 'where will my add land' (`task-mgr current`)
  9. switch active PRD (export TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX=...)
  10. view active PRD (`task-mgr current`)

DO: apply learning 1559 - validate `--depended-on-by` target's PRD BEFORE any DB write. If the target's prefix doesn't match the active prefix's PRD, emit a worked refusal that includes the exact env-var-and-rerun command.

DO NOT: use LLM / embedding lookup for intents - keep it static, deterministic, fast. Do NOT auto-redirect on cross-PRD mismatch (user explicitly chose hard-refuse). Do NOT regress the existing same-PRD --depended-on-by behavior.

modifiesBehavior: TRUE - silent landing -> hard error. Search for callers of `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by` in:
  - tests/*.rs (assert_cmd invocations)
  - .task-mgr/tasks/*-prompt.md (existing loop prompts may rely on the silent behavior)
  - scripts/ and any shell history fixtures
Each caller affected must be documented in the progress log; either update the caller or assert in a test that the new behavior matches expectations.

Edge cases:
- `how ''` (empty query): list all intent keywords, exit 0
- `how` query matching multiple intents: print all matches separated by `---`, exit 0
- Cross-PRD ref with no active prefix resolved: refuse with 'no active PRD; set TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX or pass --from-json'
- --depended-on-by references an id whose prefix is in NO known PRD: suggest `task-mgr list` and refuse
edgeCases: how '' (empty query): list available intents, exit 0
how query matching multiple intents: print all matches separated by ---, exit 0
Cross-PRD ref with no active prefix resolved: refuse with 'no active PRD; set TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX or pass --from-json'

--depended-on-by id with unknown prefix (orphan/typo): suggest `task-mgr list --prefix

` and refuse
Existing test fixtures in tests/cli_tests.rs that depend on silent-landing behavior: must be identified and updated

failureModes.cause: --depended-on-by id has prefix matching no known PRD

userStories.description:
Review all FEAT-001..004 implementations for refactoring opportunities. Specifically look for:

1. Duplication across current.rs / cheatsheet.rs / how.rs - all three render text from static data. If two of them grew similar formatting helpers, extract a shared module.
2. Shared helpers between error_recovery.rs (wrong-name/flag hints) and intents.rs (how recipes) - both are 'lookup hand-curated string by key'. Don't merge if the data shapes differ; do merge if a common Lookup<Key, Text> trait emerged.
3. ResolvedContext consumers (FEAT-001 in add.rs, FEAT-004 cross-PRD check) - verify they call the SAME helper, not parallel impls.
4. introspect.rs callers (cheatsheet, enhance/templates, drift test) - verify single source of truth.
5. New subcommands (current, cheatsheet, how) - all 3 follow the same 6-file shape per learning 1405. Verify consistency.

DO: invoke /simplify on any single touchpoint that's ugly (don't wait for review-spawn churn). Spawn REFACTOR-FIX-xxx via `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` for cross-cutting refactors only.

DO NOT: refactor for hypothetical future use; only for current-code DRY/clarity.
userStories.notes: Spawn fixes via `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` for atomic DB+JSON sync. Use /simplify directly on isolated ugly touchpoints. Reuse helpers from src/loop_engine/prompt_sections/ (small static composers) if the shape matches.

userStories.description:
Final code review. RUNS THE FULL QUALITY GATE (unscoped cargo test, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check). Replays the failure-trace from the plan Context section as an end-to-end smoke.

DO: check every new subcommand is reachable via `task-mgr --help`; new modules registered in handlers.rs and main.rs; cli/tests.rs exhaustive match updated. Run FULL cargo test (no scope filter) - this is THE place the full suite runs.

DO: end-to-end smoke verification:
  1. `task-mgr set-status FOO done` -> exits non-zero with worked hint (FEAT-003)
  2. `TASK_MGR_ACTIVE_PREFIX=stale task-mgr add --depended-on-by <foreign-prd-id> '...'` -> refuses, no JSON written, no DB row added (FEAT-004)
  3. `task-mgr cheatsheet` -> shows only real commands; introducing `set-status` reference into curated recipes fails CI (FEAT-002)
  4. `task-mgr current` -> reports prefix + source + path (FEAT-001)
  5. `task-mgr how 'change task status'` -> returns transition table (FEAT-004)
  6. Drift test: introduce a fake `--top-k` reference into the curated recipes block -> tests/cheatsheet_drift.rs fails

DO: documentation - update the managed `## task-mgr workflow` block in project CLAUDE.md to reference `current`, `cheatsheet`, and `how`. Note in progress log that the global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md edit (removing `set-status` reference) is the USER'S responsibility - flag it as a follow-up.

DO NOT: skip the wiring check - unconnected code is the #1 failure mode. Do NOT scope the test suite (no `cargo test -p <crate>` - this task runs the unscoped suite).
userStories.notes: Spawn fixes via `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` for atomic DB+JSON sync. The end-to-end smokes are the contractual replay of the failure trace from /home/chris/.claude/plans/plan-out-how-to-lazy-pixel.md (Context section). If implementation drifted from plan, update remaining tasks via `task-mgr loop init tasks/agent-learnability.json --append --update-existing`.
